docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING MODIFIED AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER ISOM REESE'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FROM THE SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S LICENSE IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 15, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE RESPONDENT THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE RESPONDENT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 15, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER REESE III, ISOM AS TO RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT FILED BY PETITIONER REESE III, ISOM SERVED SEP-17-2012, MAIL AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT, ACKNOWLEDGMENT DATE NOV-26-2012 AS TO RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA
EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER STAY OF SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S CDL; DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER REESE III, ISOM

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUEMNT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER ISOM REESE'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FROM THE SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S LICENSE IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS EXERCISED INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN REVIEWING THE DECISION BELOW. UPON SUCH REVIEW, THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD DOES NOT SUPPORT A FINDING THAT AT THE TIME OF ARREST THE ARRESTING OFFICER HAD PROBABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT THE PETITIONER WAS DRIVING HIS VEHICLE IN VIOLATION OF VEHICLE CODE 23154. THAT FINDING, HOWEVER, IS NOT RELEVANT TO THE DECISION BELOW BECAUSE THERE WAS PROBABLE CAUSE TO ARREST THE PETITIONER FOR VIOLATION OF VEHICLE CODE 23152, WHICH JUSTIFIED THE OTHER FINDING MADE BY THE HEARING OFFICER, WHICH OTHER FINDINGS ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate Form The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
E. Michael Linscheid, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael Linscheid, Tel. 415.782.6002, for petitioner and moving party;
Robert Andrew Harkness, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2216, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of arguemnt, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ISOM REESE's Petition For Writ Of Mandate From The Suspension Of Petitioner's California Driver'S License is denied. The Court has exercised independent judgment in reviewing the decision below. Upon such review, the Court determines that the administrative record does not support a finding that at the time of arrest the arresting officer had probable cause to believe that the Petitioner was driving his vehicle in violation of vehicle code 23154. That finding, however, is not relevant to the decision below because there was probable cause to arrest the Petitioner for violation of Vehicle Code 23152, which justified the other finding made by the hearing officer, which other findings are sufficient to support the administrative decision. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate Form The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License
